title: An Object I Use Every Day
summary: Describe one small everyday object — what it looks like, where it lives, and why you use it — using present simple.
topic: A small object I use every day
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences describing one small object you use every day. Say what it looks like, where you keep it, how you use it, and why it matters to you. Use present simple verbs.
sampleText: I have a small blue notebook that I carry everywhere. It fits in the front pocket of my bag. The cover is plain and a little worn because I use it every day. Inside, the pages are white and narrow. I use this notebook to write down things I need to remember. I sometimes scribble a word I hear, a name, or a small idea. I always keep a pen next to it in the same pocket. When I am on the bus or in a waiting room, I open the notebook and read my old notes. I never use my phone for these small things because the notebook is faster for me. I started this notebook three months ago and it is nearly full. I feel a little sad when a notebook ends. I always buy the same kind because I know it works for me. It is a very ordinary object but I miss it when I forget it at home.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đơn giản và hơi cũ mòn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: write down things I need to remember
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: viết lại những điều cần nhớ
viết vội một từ tôi nghe được

sampleAnnotations.phrase: keep a pen next to it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: để một cái bút ngay bên cạnh
đọc lại những ghi chú cũ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: feel a little sad when a notebook ends
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy hơi buồn khi dùng hết một cuốn sổ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: miss it when I forget it at home
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhớ nó khi bỏ quên ở nhà

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple to describe an object and its use
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để mô tả một đồ vật và cách bạn dùng nó: 'It fits', 'I carry', 'I keep', 'I use'. Chú ý động từ 'have' không thêm -s khi chủ ngữ là 'I': 'I have a notebook'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to describe an object and how you use it: 'It fits', 'I carry', 'I keep', 'I use'. Note that 'have' with 'I' is just 'have': 'I have a notebook'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi có một quyển sổ tay nhỏ màu xanh mà tôi mang theo khắp nơi. Nó vừa vặn trong túi trước của ba lô. Bìa đơn giản và hơi cũ mòn vì tôi dùng nó mỗi ngày. Bên trong, các trang giấy trắng và hẹp. Tôi dùng quyển sổ này để ghi lại những điều cần nhớ. Đôi khi tôi viết vội một từ nghe được, một cái tên, hay một ý nhỏ. Tôi luôn để một cái bút ngay bên cạnh nó trong cùng một túi. Khi trên xe buýt hay trong phòng chờ, tôi mở sổ ra và đọc lại những ghi chú cũ. Tôi không bao giờ dùng điện thoại cho những việc nhỏ này vì quyển sổ tiện hơn đối với tôi. Tôi bắt đầu quyển sổ này ba tháng trước và nó gần đầy rồi. Tôi cảm thấy hơi buồn khi dùng hết một cuốn sổ. Tôi luôn mua cùng loại vì tôi biết nó phù hợp với mình. Đây là một vật rất bình thường nhưng tôi nhớ nó khi bỏ quên ở nhà.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses present simple to describe an object?
options: It fitted in the front pocket of my bag.
It is fitting in the front pocket of my bag.
It fits in the front pocket of my bag.
It will fit in the front pocket of my bag.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'it fits' describes the object's regular property — where it always fits.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense describes a permanent or regular feature of an object?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'I ___ a small notebook.'
options: haves
having
have
had
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I', the verb 'have' does not change: 'I have'.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'have' add -s when the subject is 'I'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' to explain a preference?
options: I use the notebook because faster.
I use the notebook because it is faster for me.
Because faster I use the notebook.
I use the notebook because to be faster.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because it is faster for me' — a full clause with subject + verb + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a complete clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to contrast an ordinary object with its emotional importance?
options: It is ordinary and I miss it when I forget it.
It is ordinary because I miss it when I forget it.
It is ordinary but I miss it when I forget it.
It is ordinary so I miss it when I forget it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'ordinary but I miss it' — 'but' contrasts the object's plain appearance with its personal importance.
mcQuestions.hint: Which connector shows that something ordinary is also unexpectedly important?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'always' in a present simple sentence?
options: I always keep a pen next to it.
I keep always a pen next to it.
Always I keep a pen next to it.
I keep a pen always next to it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb 'keep': 'I always keep'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb of frequency goes directly before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'sometimes' in the correct position?
options: I sometimes scribble a word I hear.
I scribble sometimes a word I hear.
Sometimes I scribble a always word.
I scribble a word I hear sometimes always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'sometimes' can go before the main verb: 'I sometimes scribble'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'sometimes' go in this sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct word: 'The cover is plain and a little ___.'
options: new
clean
worn
loud
mcQuestions.explanation: 'worn' describes something old and slightly damaged from regular use — natural for a notebook used every day.
mcQuestions.hint: What word describes something that looks old because you use it a lot?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which negative sentence is correct in the present simple?
options: I not use my phone for these things.
I never use my phone for these things.
I no use my phone for these things.
I am not use my phone for these things.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I never use' is the correct present simple negative using 'never' before the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Which form makes a correct present simple negative?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes a past action with a past simple verb?
options: I start this notebook three months ago.
I starting this notebook three months ago.
I started this notebook three months ago.
I starts this notebook three months ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'I started' — a regular verb adds -ed: 'started'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past simple form of 'start'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means an object is almost completely full?
options: nearly empty
nearly full
quite new
quite clean
mcQuestions.explanation: 'nearly full' means there is very little space left — correct for a notebook with few blank pages.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes something that has almost no space left?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'when' correctly to describe a condition?
options: I miss it when I forget it at home.
I miss it because when forget at home.
When I miss it forget at home.
I miss it when at home forget it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'when I forget it at home' — 'when' introduces a time clause with subject + verb.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'when', you need a subject and a verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a small object you use every day that you would miss if it broke or got lost? Why do ordinary objects sometimes feel important?
